title: pseudo-fumaria major
intro: Pseudo-fumaria major, also known as False Fumitory, is a perennial plant species in the family Papaveraceae, characterized by its yellowish-green flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Pseudo-fumaria major are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Pseudo-fumaria major is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is commonly used in traditional medicine, particularly in European folk remedies.
